description: stadium in Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: The Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum (also known as the Los Angeles Coliseum or L.A. Coliseum) is a multi-purpose stadium in the Exposition Park neighborhood of Los Angeles, California, United States.

description: former movie theater in downtown Los Angeles, California, United States
description_wiki: Paramount Theatre (formerly Metropolitan Theater or Grauman's Metropolitan Theater; also known as Paramount Downtown) was a movie palace and office building located at 323 W 6th Street and 536 S Hill Street, across the street from Pershing Square, in the historic core of downtown Los Angeles.

description: historic department store building in Los Angeles, California, United States

description: former U.S. Numbered Highway in the United States
description_wiki: U.S. Route 66 or U.S. Highway 66 (US 66 or Route 66) was one of the original highways in the United States Numbered Highway System.

description: city in and county seat of McKinley County, New Mexico, United States
description_wiki: Gallup is a city in McKinley County, New Mexico, United States, with a population of 21,899 as of the 2020 census.

description: Methodist missionary and theologian (1884-1973)
description_wiki: Eli Stanley Jones (1884–1973) was an American Methodist Christian missionary, theologian, and author.

largest Christian Church, made up of 24 autonomous Churches in communion with the Pope, part of the Latin Church aka Latin Christianity aka western Christianity
description_wiki: The Catholic Church (Latin: Ecclesia Catholica), commonly called the Roman Catholic Church, is the largest Christian church, with an estimated 1.28 to 1.41 billion baptized members worldwide as of 2026.

description: American orchestra based in Los Angeles, California
description_wiki: The Los Angeles Philharmonic (LA Phil) is an American orchestra based in Los Angeles, California.

description: open-air theatre in Hollywood, Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: The Hollywood Bowl is an amphitheatre and public park in the Hollywood Hills of Los Angeles, California.

description: United States government New Deal agency (1935–1939) employing millions of unemployed people to carry out public works projects
description_wiki: The Works Progress Administration (WPA; from 1935 to 1939, then known as the Work Projects Administration from 1939 to 1943) was an American New Deal agency that employed millions of jobseekers (mostly men who were not formally educated) to carry out public works projects, including the construction of public buildings and roads.

aliases: The Holy Orthodox Catholic Apostolic Eastern Church
description: second-largest Christian church
description_wiki: The Eastern Orthodox Church, officially the Orthodox Catholic Church, also known as the Greek Orthodox Church or simply the Orthodox Church, is a Christian communion of autocephalous national and regional Eastern Christian churches that adhere to Dyophysite Christology.

description: Christian commemoration of the resurrection of Jesus
description_wiki: Easter, also called Pasch () or Pascha or Resurrection Sunday, is a Christian festival and cultural holiday commemorating the resurrection of Jesus from the dead, described in the Bible's New Testament as having occurred on the third day of his burial following his crucifixion by the Romans at Calvary c. 30 AD. It is the culmination of the Passion of Jesus, preceded by Lent (or Great Lent), a 40-day period of fasting, prayer, and penance.

description: national park in the Sierra Nevada mountains, California, U.S.
description_wiki: Sequoia National Park is a national park of the United States in the southern Sierra Nevada east of Visalia, California.

description: national park in California, United States
description_wiki: Yosemite National Park ( yoh-SEM-ih-tee) is a national park of the United States in California.

description: American automobile company
description_wiki: The Jewett was an automobile built in Detroit, Michigan, United States by the Paige-Detroit Motor Car Company from  March 1922 until December 1926.

description: American car (1908-1927)
description_wiki: The Ford Model T is an automobile that was produced by the Ford Motor Company from October 1, 1908, to May 26, 1927.

description: American automobile division of GM
description_wiki: Chevrolet is an American automobile division of the manufacturer General Motors (GM).

description: county seat of Fresno County, California, United States
description_wiki: Fresno ( ; Spanish for 'ash tree') is a city in the San Joaquin Valley of California, United States.

description: worldwide economic depression (1929–1939)
description_wiki: The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

aliases: Republican Party of the United States
description: American political party
description_wiki: The Republican Party, commonly known as the Grand Old Party (GOP), is the major conservative and right-wing political party in the United States.

description: medical practice and medical research group based in Rochester, Minnesota, United States
description_wiki: Mayo Clinic ( MAY-oh) is a private American academic medical center focused on integrated healthcare, education, and research.

description: Viennese waltz by Johann Strauss II
description_wiki:
